concerns.text: Constitution Principle VI requires that any discrepancy greater than an absolute 0.05 be flagged. The plan’s FR‑004 tolerance uses `max(0.01, 0.2 × reconstructed_p)` (and a 5 % effect‑size tolerance), which can permit discrepancies larger than 0.05 (e.g., for reconstructed p = 0.8 the allowed difference is 0.16). This conflicts with the constitution’s stricter absolute‑difference rule.
